access: local route = kong.router.get_route()local service = kong.router.get_service()-- Hardcoded scope policy map-- Route name takes precedence over service namelocal scope_policy_map = {  -- Route-level policies  ["migration-check"] = { "vip" },  ["payments-route"] = { "premium" },  -- Service-level policies  ["mig-service"] = { "vip" },  ["customer-service"] = { "basic", "vip" },  ["billing-service"] = { "premium" }}local allowed_scopes = nillocal policy_key = nil-- Route-level policy takes precedenceif route and route.name and type(scope_policy_map[route.name]) == "table" then  allowed_scopes = scope_policy_map[route.name]  policy_key = route.nameelseif service and service.name and type(scope_policy_map[service.name]) == "table" then  allowed_scopes = scope_policy_map[service.name]  policy_key = service.nameelse  -- No scope policy configured for this route/service  returnendlocal scope_header = kong.request.get_header("x-authenticated-scope")if not scope_header or scope_header == "" then  return kong.response.exit(403, {    message = "Missing authenticated scope"  })end-- Convert allowed scopes array into lookup setlocal allowed_scope_set = {}for _, s in ipairs(allowed_scopes) do  if type(s) == "string" and s ~= "" then    allowed_scope_set[s] = true  endendlocal matched = falsefor s in string.gmatch(scope_header, "([^,%s]+)") do  if allowed_scope_set[s] then    matched = true    break  endendif not matched then  return kong.response.exit(403, {    message = "Insufficient scope for policy key: " .. policy_key  })end
